Ad Text:
Morse Micro is at the forefront of next-generation Wi-Fi technology, delivering breakthrough solutions that redefine wireless connectivity. Our innovative products are designed to meet the demands of the rapidly evolving market, providing superior performance and reliability.


About the Role


Based in Sydney and reporting to senior engineering leadership, you will lead our Device Verification & Test team. This team characterises and qualifies Morse Micro’s Wi-Fi HaLow silicon, modules, and reference designs across RF performance, throughput-vs-range, power consumption, sleep behaviour, interference resilience, and stability across voltage and temperature corners.

This is a hands-on engineering leadership role. You will set the DVT roadmap, guide technical execution, and ensure the team’s data supports datasheets, production screening limits, customer commitments, and product release decisions. You will work closely with Hardware, Systems, Software, Software Validation, Product Engineering, and Operations to keep validation aligned with chip programs, module releases, and software release cadences.

You will also own the bridge from characterisation to production test, leading our ATE test development program and supporting load board qualification, NPI handoff, and high-volume manufacturing readiness.

We are also looking for someone excited about using AI tooling pragmatically to improve validation productivity across test automation, data analysis, anomaly detection, and report generation.


Key Responsibilities


Lead the DVT team and own its roadmap, aligning priorities with silicon programs, module releases, reference designs, and software release cadences.

Drive characterisation and qualification of new silicon, modules, and reference designs after hardware bring-up, including RF, throughput, power, sleep, interference, and stability testing.

Establish sample coverage, characterise spread, set datasheet and production-screening limits, and provide clear recommendations for release decisions.

Maintain device-level regression coverage so firmware, driver, hardware, and configuration changes do not silently shift RF performance, power consumption, or link behaviour.

Partner with Hardware, Systems, Software, Software Validation, Product Engineering, and Operations on product validation, release readiness, and NPI execution.

Oversee ATE test development for RF SoCs, including test program development, load board qualification, correlation, and production handoff.

Improve validation throughput using automation, CI, dashboards, structured data stores, and AI-assisted analysis and reporting.

Maintain and evolve the team’s test infrastructure, including LitePoint-based RF setups, lab automation, Jenkins CI, DVT dashboards, and test data pipelines.

Support failure analysis on customer returns and feed findings back into design, validation, and production test flows.

Set priorities, manage workload, and communicate outcomes, risks, and recommendations clearly to engineering leadership.

Required Skills & Experience


BS/MS in Electrical Engineering, Systems Engineering, or a related discipline.

10+ years in semiconductor, RF, wireless, or connected-product validation, including 3+ years leading an engineering team.

Strong grounding in RF and wireless test fundamentals, including Tx/Rx characterisation, EVM, sensitivity, current consumption, interference testing, and over-the-air measurements.

Hands-on RF hardware experience, including RF circuit fundamentals, matching networks, antenna integration, and interpreting how design choices appear in measured results.

Demonstrated experience validating complex wireless products through characterisation, qualification, and release.

Experience with lab equipment such as LitePoint, spectrum analysers, signal generators, VNAs, power meters, and automated test setups.

Strong Python test automation experience.

Track record scaling validation automation using CI, Jenkins or equivalent infrastructure, data pipelines, dashboards, and structured reporting.

Proven ability to work across Hardware, Systems, Software, Software Validation, Product Engineering, and Operations to drive validation outcomes.

Excellent written and verbal communication, with the ability to frame tests clearly, explain trade-offs, and present recommendations to engineering leadership.

Interest in applying AI tooling to test automation, data analysis, anomaly detection, and reporting.

Desirable


Background in Wi-Fi or related wireless standards.

ATE test program development for RF ICs (Teradyne UltraFLEX or similar).

Experience designing or reviewing RF load boards.

Familiarity with IC production test flows and the NPI process.

High-volume production testing, yield analysis, and characterisation reporting.

Data analysis tools — Python, JMP, or equivalent.

Experience with relational data stores (e.g. MySQL) for managing test data at scale.

What we offer


Competitive salary + excellent stock option package

Performance bonus opportunity

Income protection insurance

Healthy work environment with sit/stand desks and large screens

Lots of snacks & drinks, including barista coffee, Friday team lunches & some of the world’s best beers

Flexible working hours

Work from home policy

Community & social groups and much more

Join a high performing, inclusive company where you can make a real impact

Who we are


Morse Micro is Australia’s largest semiconductor company building Wi-Fi HaLow (802.11ah) chips for the Internet of Things (IoT). We are a team of wireless experts that love to work hard, innovate, and invent. Together, we are building the world’s lowest power Wi-Fi technology that will enable billions of IoT devices to connect securely to the internet. We are a global team with offices in Sydney, Picton & Melbourne (Australia), Irvine, Bay Area & Boston (USA), Bangalore (India), Cambridge (UK), Hangzhou (China), Taipei (Taiwan) and Tokyo (Japan).

Apply for this Job

About

Driven by our AI-first strategy, we are creating AI-native solutions and an AI-empowered workforce to deliver best-in-class experiences for over 2 million Australians. You will join our talented Enterprise AI Enablement team to build leading-edge solutions within our agentic AI ecosystem.

What Role Will You Play?

With a strong emphasis on software engineering excellence, you will spend the majority of your time writing production-grade code and automating complex workflows to create a resilient foundation for our agentic AI ecosystem.

Design, architect, build, test, and operate scalable backend systems

Implement LLMOps best practices for deploying and monitoring AI models
Build reliable, observable, and production-ready AI platforms

This role is ideal for a software engineer who has transitioned into Data/ML & AI and wants to build enterprise-grade AI systems at scale.

What You Offer

Experience across the full SDLC: design, build, test, and support mission-critical production platforms

Experience managing deployments in containerised cloud environments (Docker/Kubernetes)

RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation)
MCP

Google ADK
LangChain / LangGraph
LlamaIndex

If you feel you possess the relevant skills to the above, apply now with most updated CV!

The Role

We're hiring a Data Engineer to manage our data pipelines for ML. This covers data collection from the front-end, data generation with the ML team, dataset preparation for human labelling in Prodigy, and evaluation. We already have pipelines in place — your job is to automate them and make them reliable as we scale.

What You'll Do

- Mature and automate our data lake. We already have pipelines and storage in place — your job is to take what exists, make it robust, automated, and observable, and evolve the schemas, partitioning, and access patterns as we scale.

- Automate and harden our Python data pipelines for MLOps. Take the pipelines our team has already built, automate them end-to-end, and make them reproducible, versioned, and observable. Pipelines should reliably move data from the front-end through transformation, generation, labelling, and evaluation without manual hand-holding.

- Run our data generation workflows. Partner with the ML team to design and operate synthetic data generation pipelines — running model calls, managing prompts, capturing outputs, and tracking lineage.

- Curate datasets for human labelling in Prodigy. Prepare, sample, and stratify datasets for our annotators. Build the tooling and recipes that let our team label efficiently and consistently.

- Build evaluation pipelines. Create the harness that lets us measure whether our generated and labelled data is actually good — agreement metrics, drift detection, sample quality checks, and feedback loops back into generation.

- Close the loop. Make sure insights from evaluation flow back into how we generate, label, and select data — so each cycle is better than the last.

- Be a good data citizen. Set up monitoring, documentation, and access controls appropriate for handling student data in a privacy-sensitive context.

What You'll Bring

- 3+ years of professional experience building data pipelines or data platforms in Python.

- Solid grasp of modern data lake / lakehouse architectures on Azure and orchestration tools.

- Experience working with ML teams — you understand training data, eval sets, and why reproducibility matters.

- Hands-on experience with at least one human annotation tool.

- Hands-on experience with a modern data and ML stack — a mix of Azure services (e.g. Azure Data Lake Storage, Databricks, Azure ML, Azure Functions, Key Vault) and open source tools (e.g. Airflow, Dagster, MLflow, Delta Lake, dbt).

- A pragmatic, start-up mindset — you can scope a v1, ship it, and iterate, rather than over-engineering.

- Strong written communication. You'll be documenting systems that researchers, engineers, and annotators all rely on.

Nice to Have

- Experience with LLM-driven data generation pipelines (prompt management, output validation, cost/latency monitoring).

- Familiarity with evaluation frameworks for generative models (rubric-based eval, LLM-as-judge).

- Background in NLP, education technology, or any domain involving unstructured text data.

- Experience with handwriting recognition / OCR pipelines.

- Exposure to privacy frameworks relevant to student data (Australian Privacy Principles, GDPR, FERPA).

The Clean Energy Regulator

We are an economic regulator accelerating carbon abatement for Australia. We do this by administering Australian Government schemes to measure, manage, reduce and offset carbon emissions in Australia.

Our role is determined by climate change law. We administer schemes that contribute to the Australian Government climate target of reducing emissions to 43% below 2005 levels by 2030 and Net Zero by 2050.

Find out more about the Clean Energy Regulator and what we have to offer, please visit our website and for the latest update on activities, you can follow us on LinkedIn.

Work with us

Working with us isn’t just a job, it's a chance to have a rewarding career. We're looking for people with diverse skills, expertise and experience to work with us to reduce carbon emissions and increase the use of clean energy in Australia.

About the Business Unit

The Artificial Intelligence (AI) function will be a key enabler to the Clean Energy Regulator’s (CER) digital transformation, driving the responsible, ethical, and effective use of AI to enhance regulatory, operational efficiencies and policy outcomes. Under the leadership of the Chief AI Officer, the team will support AI usage across the Agency, aligned with the Agency’s strategic objectives and whole-of-government digital priorities.

With expertise in artificial intelligence principles, practices, and technologies, the team ensures AI initiatives are designed and deployed in ways that are scalable, secure, and aligned with the Agency’s values and operational goals. This includes applying robust AI governance frameworks, ensuring model integrity and performance, and integrating AI capabilities into core business processes to support sustainable and responsible innovation. The team operates in alignment with Agency program and BAU investment prioritisation and IT and data architecture governance arrangements.

The AI Team is structured around two teams:

AI Technical Capability Development

This section leads the design, development, and deployment of AI solutions across the Agency. It provides technical leadership, supports pilot programs, and ensures alignment with IT architecture and data governance. The team is responsible for delivering robust solutions, developing and refining AI models, and integrating AI platforms into the Agency’s digital environment. Their work ensures that AI systems are secure, scalable, and aligned with CER architecture standards, supporting both operational efficiency and innovation.

AI Adoption

This section focuses on enabling the workforce to confidently and ethically engage with AI technologies. It delivers training, builds communities of practice, and supports the identification, definition, and development of AI use cases through structured business analysis, process mapping, and stakeholder engagement. This section ensures AI is adopted in a way that is inclusive, people-centric, and aligned with the Agency’s values and operational goals.

Together, these teams support the Chief AI Officer in delivering strategic leadership, uplifting AI literacy, managing risk, and ensuring the Agency remains adaptive and innovative.

About the Section

This section focuses on enabling the workforce to confidently and ethically engage with AI technologies. It delivers training, builds communities of practice, and supports the identification, analysis, and development of AI use cases through structured business analysis, stakeholder engagement, and evidence-based decision-making, ensuring AI is adopted in a way that is inclusive, people-centric, and aligned with the Agency’s values and operational goals.

The AI Adoption Section plays a key role in embedding artificial intelligence into the Clean Energy Regulator’s culture, systems, and services. Focused on people and purpose, the section supports the Agency’s transition to smarter, more responsive ways of working by making AI accessible, trusted, and impactful.

Focus:

Empowering Staff: Building confidence and capability across the Agency by supporting staff to understand, engage with, and benefit from AI tools in their daily work.
Collaborative Delivery: Working closely with business areas to analyse operational needs, define requirements, and co-design AI solutions that deliver measurable improvements in decision-making and efficiency.
Inclusive Innovation: Bringing together a multidisciplinary team of professionals and domain experts to ensure solutions are practical, ethical, and aligned with the Agency’s values and strategic direction.
Partnerships Across Government: Collaborating with other Australian Government agencies to share insights, align approaches, and contribute to a coordinated public sector AI strategy.
Supporting Change: Leading change management efforts by ensuring solutions are informed by clear requirements, business impacts, and user needs, enabling smooth and sustainable AI adoption.

About the Role

The AI Enablement Lead supports the implementation of strategic initiatives to integrate artificial intelligence (AI) technologies across the agency, ensuring alignment with government priorities, ethical standards, and operational goals. This role involves stakeholder engagement, change management, and cross-functional coordination to harness AI opportunities and drive innovation.  It also has a focus on managing the lifecycle of AI initiatives and ensuring business process re-engineering work informs efficiency or quality improvements for existing Agency processes.

How to Apply

To learn more about this role, please click on 'Apply' to view the Clean Energy Regulator Careers page.

Other Benefits

Great work-life balance.
Competitive salary packaging and leave options, including purchase leave.
Diverse, supportive, inclusive and agile work environment, with staff at all levels being part of conversations with the senior executive and being offered the opportunity to experience different types of work.
A wide range of learning and development opportunities.

Job Title: Senior Cloud Engineer (1 x Perm, 1 x 18m Fixed Term Contract)
Ad Text:
Novigi is the data and technology partner to the Financial Services Industry with offices in Sydney, Brisbane, Canberra, Melbourne, Wollongong and Colombo, Sri Lanka. At our core, we are data-driven transformation and business optimisation experts. We partner with our clients to transform their business functions through the clever use of data and interoperability. We do this by offering a range of services and solutions.

Description

About the Role:

We are seeking an experienced and highly capable Senior Cloud Engineer to join our team and play a pivotal role in shaping the future of our cloud infrastructure. This is a senior individual contributor position with significant influence over technical direction, operating at the intersection of two mature cloud disciplines: sustaining and elevating a well-established AWS environment, and driving the uplift of our Azure footprint to meet the same standard of reliability, security, and operational maturity.

This role is suited to someone who is equally comfortable governing a complex production AWS estate as they are designing and building Azure environments from the ground up — bringing structure, best practice, and engineering rigour to both.

What we are looking for:

You bring deep, hands-on experience across both AWS and Azure — not just familiarity, but the ability to architect, build, troubleshoot, and govern at an engineering level on both platforms. Specifically, you will have:

Proven experience managing and maturing a production-grade AWS environment, including account structure, IAM, networking, and operational controls
Demonstrated ability to review and provide input into designs and implement Azure environments from a foundational level, including Azure Landing Zones, Entra ID integration, policy-driven governance, and network architecture
Strong, practical Terraform skills — writing reusable modules, managing remote state, and integrating IaC pipelines into CI/CD workflows (experience with tools such as Buildkite, GitHub Actions, or Azure DevOps is advantageous)
Experience operating in a financial services or similarly regulated industry, with an understanding of the compliance and audit requirements that come with it
The ability to think strategically about platform design while remaining hands-on in delivery
At this level, the successful candidate is expected to operate as a technical lead - providing hands-on guidance, coaching, and mentoring to the broader team, and elevating the capability of those around them alongside their own delivery output.

Qualifications/Experience:

Essential

8+ years of experience in cloud engineering or infrastructure roles, with at least 5 years working across both AWS and Azure in a hands-on capacity
AWS certifications at Associate level or above (Solutions Architect, DevOps Engineer, or equivalent)
Microsoft Azure certifications at Associate level or above (AZ-104, AZ-305, or equivalent)
Demonstrated experience owning and maturing a production AWS environment at scale, including account governance, IAM, networking (VPC, Transit Gateway, Direct Connect), and cost management
Proven experience designing and deploying Azure platform foundations, including Azure Landing Zones, hub-and-spoke networking, Entra ID / Azure AD, Azure Policy, and Management Group hierarchy
Strong, production-grade Terraform skills — module development, remote state management, workspace strategies, and pipeline-integrated deployments
Experience integrating IaC workflows into CI/CD platforms such as GitHub Actions, Buildkite, or Azure DevOps
Solid understanding of cloud security principles, including identity and access management, network segmentation, secrets management, and audit logging across both platforms
Experience working in a regulated industry (financial services, superannuation, healthcare, or similar), with familiarity with compliance frameworks such as APRA CPS 234, ISO 27001, IRAP ISM, or SOC 2

Highly Desirable

Experience with cloud security tooling such as AWS Security Hub, Microsoft Defender for Cloud, or equivalent CSPM platforms
Familiarity with FinOps practices and cloud cost governance tooling
Exposure to Netskope, or other cloud-delivered security platforms
Experience with monitoring and observability platforms (CloudWatch, Azure Monitor, Datadog, or similar)
Prior experience in a principal, lead, or senior individual contributor capacity, including contributing to architecture decision records and technical standards.
Practical experience leveraging AI tooling, including large language model assistants such as Claude, to accelerate cloud engineering delivery, improve code and IaC quality, support documentation, and enhance problem-solving across the platform engineering lifecycle. Candidates who actively incorporate AI into their day-to-day engineering workflow and can demonstrate tangible quality or productivity outcomes will be highly regarded.

Why our clients love Novigi:

Novigi moves quickly, thinks sharply, and delivers what’s been agreed. Clients tell us we’re easy to work with because we stay focused on outcomes, cut through complexity, and keep communication open and honest. Behind that simplicity sits a huge depth of industry expertise and serious technical capability, built from years of working across superannuation, data, integration, and complex platforms. Our culture of innovation, collaboration and banter shows up in how we work day to day: teams that slot in seamlessly, challenge constructively, adapt as needs shift, and stay committed to getting the right things done well.

Only those candidates chosen to proceed will be contacted.

With board backing and budget committed, the team has moved beyond experimentation. Live use cases are in production, and there is now a genuine pipeline of workflow problems the business wants solved through agentic AI.
  
You won't be joining a mature function to optimise around the edges. You'll help shape what the AI capability becomes, how it gets built, and where it creates the most value across the business.
  
Reporting directly to the CIO, you'll turn that early success into scalable AI systems.
  
The Role:

This is not a classic ML or Data Science role. It’s a hands-on senior IC role for someone who can take a real business problem, apply agentic AI in practice, and ship solutions that deliver value.
That could be a Microsoft-first full-stack engineer who has moved into AI agents and applied AI systems. Or it could be a Power Platform developer who has expanded into AI, automation, and workflow delivery.  
What you’ll be doing:

Design and deploy AI agents and intelligent workflows across key business use cases
Build across Copilot Studio, Power Automate, Power Apps, Power BI, Dataverse, and the wider Power Platform
Develop data pipelines, semantic models, and reporting to support AI and analytics
Integrate AI solutions into core systems through APIs and Azure services
Turn successful internal use cases into repeatable, governed solutions
Work closely with stakeholders to identify workflow and automation opportunities
Ensure solutions meet privacy, governance, and audit requirements  
What We’re Looking For:

Hands-on experience building commercial AI agents, intelligent workflows, or applied AI solutions.
Strong practical capability in Copilot Studio, this is a genuine non-negotiable
Strong Microsoft ecosystem capability across Power Platform and Azure services
Experience integrating AI into real business processes, not just experimentation or proof-of-concept work
Good API and systems integration capability, ideally with some Azure Functions, PowerShell, SQL, Python, JavaScript, or similar scripting experience
Able to work end-to-end, from workflow discovery through to build, deployment, and iteration
Comfortable operating as a senior IC with a high level of autonomy
Strong communication skills and confidence working with non-technical stakeholders
Experience in financial services, professional services, or other detail-heavy environments would be valuable
Apply now or reach out for a confidential discussion.

The Company
Join a small, senior team of software engineers in a well funded company, reporting directly to the engineering co-founders. Own features and help steer the direct of our ML product used by companies in Australia and the UK. And work on a product that users love. 

About you

You're a senior software engineer who has shipped ML-powered features to real users at a commercial product company, and you can talk concretely about how they worked and what you'd do differently

Remote first, anywhere in Australia, or hybrid in Melbourne if you're local

Job Title: L3 Platform Engineer / Senior Systems Engineer
Ad Text:
We’re working on a new 6-month contract opportunity (high chance of extension) for a hands-on L3 Platform Engineer / Senior Systems Engineer to join a well-established Insurance company in North Sydney.

This role sits within a Level 3 Service Delivery environment and will suit someone who enjoys being close to the tools, owning escalations, supporting critical infrastructure, and working across both BAU and project delivery.
You’ll be working across a broad technology environment, including Azure cloud infrastructure, Windows Server, Active Directory, VMware, networking, automation and Azure DevOps.

What you’ll be doing

You’ll be responsible for supporting incidents, requests and technical changes across the infrastructure and platform environment. The role will involve troubleshooting complex issues, supporting Windows and VMware environments, assisting with root cause analysis, driving technical changes, and working closely with internal product teams, vendors and business stakeholders.

You’ll also be involved in project deliverables, automation, continuous improvement and ensuring infrastructure changes are delivered safely and in line with business and technology outcomes.

What we’re looking for

Strong experience across:

Azure infrastructure administration
Windows Server administration
Active Directory
VMware, vSphere, vCenter and ESXi
PowerShell scripting and automation
Networking fundamentals including routing, firewalls, NSGs and VLANs
Azure DevOps, including Boards, Repos and Pipelines
Infrastructure as Code exposure, ideally Bicep, ARM or Terraform
Incident support, troubleshooting and root cause analysis
BAU support and project delivery

The Role:

We are looking for a Senior Quality Engineer - AI to help shape how StarRez evaluates, validates, and improves AI-powered product experiences.

You will play a pivotal role in elevating our quality practices for AI-powered product experiences and tooling. You will be a product expert within our engineering organization, deeply understanding workflows and customer outcomes. This role will balance hands-on individual contributor responsibilities with leading, influencing, and coaching your peers.

You’re someone who is passionate about seeing systems holistically and is eager to drive the highest standards of accuracy, safety, usefulness, and reliability. You will help define what "good" AI output means for StarRez, build repeatable evaluation systems, coach reviewers and subject matter experts, and turn subjective feedback into measurable product improvement.

You will work at the intersection of AI engineering, product, and domain knowledge — partnering with engineers, product managers, and SMEs to raise the bar on how StarRez evaluates, monitors, and improves AI tools.

Key Responsibilities:

Lead or contributed to end-to-end AI quality and evaluation strategy for product experiences involving LLMs, RAG, prompts, tool-use, or agent workflows.
Provided quality-focused input during ticket grooming, discovery, and feature discussions, with clear guidance on testability, observability, scalability, reliability, product resilience, customer usability, and operational readiness.
Championed shift-left quality practices by influencing or mentoring developers and cross-functional partners on evaluation-driven development, clear acceptance criteria, early validation, and fast feedback loops.
Coached or calibrated human reviewers, subject matter experts, or product teams by running calibration sessions, reviewing patterns, improving rubric clarity, and converting domain knowledge into reliable evaluation criteria.
Contributed to the evolution of development or quality pipelines, including automated and human evaluation workflows, regression monitoring after model upgrades, prompt changes, RAG updates, or agent workflow changes.
Define practical AI quality standards, rubrics, and evaluation criteria that help teams assess AI outputs consistently across StarRez product workflows.
Partner with engineering and product teams to identify AI quality risks early, including risks related to grounding, hallucination, tool-use behaviour, retrieval quality, customer impact, and safe escalation.
Build or support repeatable evaluation approaches that help teams compare prompt, model, RAG, data, or workflow changes before release.
Translate AI failure patterns into actionable product and engineering improvements, such as prompt changes, retrieval improvements, source-data fixes, tool safeguards, UX changes, or escalation-flow updates.
Use metrics, reviewer feedback, quality trends, and operational signals to support release readiness and ongoing product improvement.

Required Experience:

Quality Assurance or Quality Engineering experience in a SaaS B2B or B2B2C company.
Proven track record as a senior individual contributor with a strong quality mindset and the ability to influence technical and product direction.
Excellent technical communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to guide engineering peers, product partners, and non-technical stakeholders through clear quality insights.
Data-driven approach to quality management, with experience using metrics, dashboards, trend analysis, reviewer agreement, or operational reporting to guide decisions.
Hands-on technical comfort with test automation, evaluation tooling, observability, or scripting, and the ability to partner with engineers to build pragmatic evaluation pipelines.

Highly regarded:

Experience with LLM evaluation pipelines, automated eval tooling, LLM judges, or agent observability platforms.
Background in traditional QA or quality engineering, particularly in environments transitioning to AI-augmented workflows.
Familiarity with safety, bias, and compliance evaluation for AI systems.
Experience in SaaS or enterprise software environments, ideally with exposure to student housing, property management, or similar domainspecific AI products.
Exposure to production monitoring of AI systems, including drift detection and post-release quality tracking.

Reasons to join our Team:

You will be part of a vibrant and supportive culture, with weekly team lunches.    
We are a flexible and hybrid workplace.   
You will have access to professional development opportunities.     
Be part of a global team, participate in monthly global meetings and partner with team members in different countries.     
Z-Factor: Our most celebrated value, you will work with a team of caring, energetic, high-performing, and passionate people who have fun supporting our vision, innovation & continuous improvement.  

We are proud of our diverse workforce and are dedicated to creating a safe and welcoming environment for all employees. People from various ethnicities, ages, genders, and abilities are encouraged to apply.

Please note, StarRez requires all candidates to have full work rights in Australia.

ABOUT THE ROLE

The Data Engineer is a hands-on role reporting to the Data Analytics Manager, responsible for designing and delivering end-to-end data pipelines across the modern data stack that support analytics and reporting. Working from ingestion through to Power BI, the role partners with global stakeholders to translate business requirements into reliable, well-structured data products.

The role also drives efficiency by leveraging AI tools such as Relevance AI and Anthropic Claude to accelerate development, documentation, and troubleshooting across the data stack.

Responsibilities

Build and maintain ingestion pipelines using Fivetran and Snowflake-native loaders to integrate data from Oracle, SaaS platforms, and external marketplaces into the warehouse
Develop Coalesce.io transformation workflows to model raw data into reliable, performant, and well-documented dimensional layers
Implement data quality controls with Coalesce.io Quality and orchestration to detect schema drift, freshness issues, and anomalies
Manage Snowflake objects (tables, views, schemas, shares), optimising performance while adhering to RBAC and cost-management practices

Create and maintain Power BI dashboards, semantic models, and DAX measures, enabling self-service analytics and delivering key dashboards.

Collaborate with global stakeholders to translate business requirements into scalable data pipelines and analytics solutions
Leverage Relevance AI and Anthropic Claude (API & MCP) to accelerate development, documentation, and troubleshooting
Develop lightweight Streamlit apps and Python utilities to improve access to data assets

Our Tech Stack

Data Platform: Snowflake, Fivetran, Coalesce.io (Transform & Quality), Apache Airflow
Business Intelligence: Power BI (semantic models, DAX, Report Builder), Power BI Service
AI / Agent Platforms: Relevance AI, Anthropic Claude (API & MCP), Claude Code, Snowflake Cortex Code
Languages: SQL, Python (pandas, snowflake-connector-python), DAX, M (Power Query)
Apps & Integration: Streamlit (Snowflake-native), REST APIs, Microsoft 365, Oracle / JDE
Collaboration: Microsoft Teams, Linear, SmartSheet

ABOUT YOU

2+ years’ experience in data, analytics, or BI engineering, delivering production data pipelines
Strong SQL skills and hands-on experience with cloud data warehouses (Snowflake preferred)
Experience with ingestion tools (Fivetran or similar) and transformation tools (Coalesce.io, dbt, etc.)
Proven Power BI expertise, including semantic modelling, DAX, and dashboard/report development
Working knowledge of Python (scripting, APIs, data validation, automation)
Ability to translate non-technical business requirements into data products and reporting solutions
Familiarity with AI/LLMs (prompt engineering, basic agent or RAG patterns)
Strong communication skills across technical and business stakeholders

Desirable Skills & Experience: 

Experience with REST APIs and MCP, ERP systems (Oracle/JDE), Snowflake Streamlit/data apps, and cloud data governance, plus exposure to retail/consumer industries or contributions to data/AI projects
